evals.prompt: Business Process: Required Minimum Distribution Processing. No existing NFRs have been defined. Sub-processes: Identify Eligibility, Calculate RMD Amount, Engage Participant, Validate Elections, Submit Disbursement, Notify Completion, Archive. Please run nfr-coverage-agent.
evals.expected_output: Coverage matrix with all 5 primary categories (Security, Performance, Usability, Availability, Disaster Recovery) showing 'None identified' for current coverage, a gap analysis for each, and 2-4 stakeholder questions per category. DR section specifically references the P0-P7 tier standard and asks which tier applies. Reference-only categories each get one status line. No NFR text written — questions only. Summary line: 5 categories with gaps.
assertions: All 5 primary NFR categories have sections in the output
Every category shows 'None identified' or equivalent for current coverage
Every category has at least 2 stakeholder questions
Disaster Recovery section asks which P0-P7 tier applies
No SLA numbers are invented in the output
No NFR requirement text is written — only questions
All 5 reference-only categories appear in the reference-only table
Summary line is present showing 5 categories with gaps

evals.prompt: Business Process: Required Minimum Distribution Processing. Existing NFRs: 'All participant PII must be encrypted at rest and in transit using AES-256.' 'The RMD calculation batch must complete within a 4-hour processing window.' Sub-processes provided. Please run nfr-coverage-agent.
evals.expected_output: Security shows 'AES-256 encryption at rest and in transit' as current coverage — but gaps identified around authorisation, access control, and audit logging. Performance shows 4-hour batch window as current coverage — but gaps around response time for participant-facing interactions. Availability, Usability, and DR all show no coverage with full question lists. Two categories (Security, Performance) show partial coverage, three show full gaps.
assertions: Security section quotes the existing AES-256 requirement as current coverage
Security section identifies gaps beyond encryption (authorisation, access control, audit)
Performance section quotes the 4-hour batch window as current coverage
Performance section identifies gaps for participant-facing response time
Usability, Availability, and DR sections all show gaps with question lists
No existing NFR text is rewritten or evaluated for quality
No SLA numbers are invented

evals.prompt: Business Process: Internal RMD Batch Processing (no user-facing interface, internal operations only). Existing NFRs: 'N/A — This is an internal-only batch process. Usability NFRs do not apply.' Sub-processes are all back-office calculation and archival steps. Please run nfr-coverage-agent.
evals.expected_output: Usability section shows 'N/A — Internal-only batch process; no user-facing interface' and does NOT generate stakeholder questions for Usability. Usability is not re-flagged in the gap analysis. All other primary categories (Security, Performance, Availability, DR) still show full assessment. Summary line correctly reflects 1 N/A, 4 with gaps.
assertions: Usability section shows the N/A status with the user-provided reason
No stakeholder questions are generated for Usability
Usability is NOT listed as a gap in the summary
Security, Performance, Availability, and DR are all assessed normally
Summary correctly counts 1 N/A category
Agent does not challenge or question the N/A acceptance
